Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Margaret Wylie papers
Wylie, Margaret, 1889-1964.
23-13-491
54.2 cubic feet. 54.2 cubic feet.
1926-1963. Extent is approximate. Correspondence, course materials, other papers. Correspondence, course outlines, trip reports, questionnaires, memoranda and other papers deriving from Dr. Wylie's professional work in child development and family studies, including twin studies and the Cornell child study clubs; includes some personal correspondence, as with Flora Rose. BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Child Development and Family Relationships, Cornell University. Dr. Wylie established the extension program in Family Life.
Margaret Wylie earned her bachelor's, master's, and doctorate degrees from the University of Michigan. She arrived at the College of Home Economics in 1925 and for three years she initiated extension institutes and follow-up programs in eight counties in New York State. She left Cornell to complete her Ph.D. in psychology and retuned in 1930 and remained until 1957. She was responsible for developing child study clubs and establishing Cornell's extension program in family life.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, course outlines, trip reports, questionnaires, memoranda and other papers deriving from Dr. Wylie's professional work in child development and family studies, including twin studies and the Cornell child study clubs; includes some personal correspondence, as with Flora Rose.
